The author, Thomas Paine, met this diplomat while in France with the pro-America Brissotists ("bree-SOH-ists"). After being sent to the U.S. by the Girondins ("zhee-ron-DENS") to arm privateers at American ports, this man was granted asylum by George Washington.
Citizen Genet's father arranged for the French edition of this Paine pamphlet, which, like The American Crisis, argues for the American Revolution.

parts: The 1794 Proclamation of Neutrality led to Thomas Jefferson resigning from this foreign-affairs related position in George Washington’s cabinet.
During the controversy, Alexander Hamilton and James Madison engaged in a series of newspaper debates referred to by their respective pen-names. Name either.

This man sparked an international incident when he asked for a suspension of American neutrality in support of France. George Washington granted this [emphasize] “Citizen” asylum after he arrived in Philadelphia.
